subject: economy
question: औद्योगिक धोरण ठराव १९४८ नुसार पूर्णतः केंद्र सरकारसाठी (सरकारी मक्तेदारी) राखीव ठेवलेले उद्योग कोणते? / As per the Industrial Policy Resolution 1948, which industries were reserved exclusively as a Central Government monopoly?
options: A. लोखंड व पोलाद, जहाजबांधणी, दूरसंचार / Iron & steel, shipbuilding, telecommunications
B. शस्त्रास्त्रे व दारूगोळा, अणुऊर्जा, रेल्वे वाहतूक / Arms & ammunition, atomic energy, railway transport
C. सिमेंट, साखर, कापड / Cement, sugar, textiles
D. खते, कोळसा, वीजनिर्मिती / Fertilizers, coal, power generation
explanation: IPR 1948 declared arms & ammunition, atomic energy and railway transport as exclusive state monopoly industries.

subject: economy
question: औद्योगिक धोरण ठराव १९५६ ने उद्योगांचे किती वर्गांमध्ये (शेड्यूल) वर्गीकरण केले? / Into how many schedules did the Industrial Policy Resolution 1956 classify industries?
options: B. तीन (अ, ब, क) / Three (A, B, C)
C. चार (अ, ब, क, ड) / Four (A, B, C, D)
explanation: IPR 1956 classified industries into Schedule A (exclusive state responsibility), Schedule B (mixed public-private) and Schedule C (private sector).

subject: economy
question: १९५६ च्या औद्योगिक धोरणातील शेड्यूल 'अ' मध्ये किती उद्योग समाविष्ट होते ज्यांची भविष्यातील वाढ पूर्णतः सरकारची जबाबदारी होती? / Under the 1956 Industrial Policy, how many industries were listed in Schedule A whose future development was the exclusive responsibility of the State?
explanation: Schedule A of IPR 1956 listed 17 industries reserved exclusively for the state.

subject: economy
question: १९९१ च्या नवीन औद्योगिक धोरणाचा मुख्य उद्देश काय होता? / What was the primary objective of the New Industrial Policy of 1991?
options: A. सर्व उद्योग राष्ट्रीयीकृत करणे / Nationalize all industries
B. औद्योगिक परवाना पद्धत रद्द करून उदारीकरण, खाजगीकरण व जागतिकीकरणास चालना देणे / Abolish industrial licensing and promote liberalization, privatization and globalization
C. लघुउद्योगांवर बंदी घालणे / Ban small scale industries
D. परकीय गुंतवणुकीवर पूर्ण बंदी घालणे / Completely ban foreign investment
explanation: The 1991 policy dismantled the License-Permit Raj, reduced public sector reservation, and opened the economy to private and foreign investment (LPG reforms).

subject: economy
question: १९९१ च्या औद्योगिक धोरणानंतर सार्वजनिक क्षेत्रासाठी राखीव उद्योगांची संख्या १७ वरून किती करण्यात आली? / After the 1991 Industrial Policy, the number of industries reserved exclusively for the public sector was reduced from 17 to how many?
explanation: The 1991 policy initially reduced the industries reserved exclusively for the public sector from 17 to 8 (later further reduced over time to just 2 - atomic energy substances and railway operations).

subject: economy
question: मक्तेदारी व निर्बंधात्मक व्यापार प्रथा (MRTP) कायदा कोणत्या वर्षी लागू करण्यात आला? / In which year was the Monopolies and Restrictive Trade Practices (MRTP) Act enacted?
explanation: The MRTP Act was enacted in 1969 to prevent concentration of economic power and monopolistic practices; it was later replaced by the Competition Act, 2002.

subject: economy
question: परकीय चलन नियमन कायदा (FERA) च्या जागी कोणता कायदा आणण्यात आला? / Which Act replaced the Foreign Exchange Regulation Act (FERA)?
options: B. परकीय चलन व्यवस्थापन कायदा (FEMA) १९९९ / Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) 1999
explanation: FERA (1973) was repealed and replaced by the more liberal FEMA in 1999, reflecting the post-1991 liberalization approach.

subject: economy
question: महाराष्ट्र औद्योगिक विकास महामंडळ (MIDC) ची स्थापना कोणत्या वर्षी झाली? / In which year was the Maharashtra Industrial Development Corporation (MIDC) established?
explanation: MIDC was established in 1962 under the Maharashtra Industrial Development Act, 1961, to develop industrial areas and infrastructure across the state.

subject: economy
question: महाराष्ट्रातील औद्योगिक प्रकल्पांना दीर्घकालीन वित्तपुरवठा करणारी 'SICOM' ही संस्था कशासाठी ओळखली जाते? / SICOM in Maharashtra is primarily known as an institution for what purpose?
options: B. औद्योगिक प्रकल्पांना वित्तीय सहाय्य व गुंतवणूक / Financial assistance and investment for industrial projects
C. शालेय शिक्षण अनुदान / School education grants
explanation: The State Industrial and Investment Corporation of Maharashtra (SICOM) provides term loans and financial assistance to promote industrial development in the state.

subject: economy
question: दुसर्या पंचवार्षिक योजनेत (१९५६-६१) महालनोबिस प्रतिकृतीनुसार कोणत्या क्षेत्रावर भर देण्यात आला? / Under the Mahalanobis model followed in the Second Five Year Plan (1956-61), which sector was given priority?
options: B. जड व मूलभूत उद्योग / Heavy and basic industries
D. लघु व कुटीर उद्योग मात्र / Small and cottage industries only
explanation: The Mahalanobis strategy in the Second Plan emphasized rapid industrialization through development of heavy and basic industries (capital goods sector).

subject: economy
question: सूक्ष्म, लघु व मध्यम उद्योग विकास (MSMED) कायदा कोणत्या वर्षी लागू करण्यात आला? / In which year was the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Development (MSMED) Act enacted?
explanation: The MSMED Act, 2006 provided the first legal framework for recognition of the concept of 'enterprise' comprising both manufacturing and service entities, classifying them as micro, small and medium.

subject: economy
question: निर्गुंतवणूक (Disinvestment) व सार्वजनिक मालमत्ता व्यवस्थापनाशी संबंधित केंद्रीय अर्थ मंत्रालयाचा विभाग कोणता? / Which department under the Union Ministry of Finance deals with disinvestment and management of public assets?
options: A. महसूल विभाग / Department of Revenue
B. गुंतवणूक व सार्वजनिक मालमत्ता व्यवस्थापन विभाग (DIPAM) / Department of Investment and Public Asset Management (DIPAM)
C. आर्थिक व्यवहार विभाग / Department of Economic Affairs
D. खर्च विभाग / Department of Expenditure
explanation: DIPAM, under the Ministry of Finance, formulates and implements the government's disinvestment policy for central public sector enterprises.
